name: JSON-LD Block Present
description: Verifies at least one valid JSON-LD script block exists. Pages with schema see 73% higher AI Overview selection, but quality matters — minimally populated schema underperforms no schema.

criteria.pass: At least one `<script type="application/ld+json">` block exists with valid JSON

fix_template: Add appropriate JSON-LD block for page type (see schema-validation.md for required fields)
sources: BrightEdge: 73% higher AI Overview selection with schema. 3.2x more AI answer appearances with FAQPage. BUT generic/minimally populated schema underperforms no schema (41.6% vs 59.8%) — quality matters.

name: @context Is https://schema.org
description: Verifies the JSON-LD @context uses the HTTPS schema.org URL. Google requires the https:// prefix; the http:// prefix may work but is technically incorrect.
sources: schema.org is the standard. Google requires `https://` prefix. HTTP prefix may work but is technically incorrect.

name: Page-Appropriate @type Present
description: Verifies the schema @type matches the page classification (e.g., Article/BlogPosting for blog pages, Product for product pages). Wrong @type means wrong interpretation by search engines and AI systems.

criteria.pass: @type matches page classification (Article/BlogPosting for blog, Product for product, etc.)
criteria.warn: @type present but may not be the best match (e.g., WebPage instead of Article for a blog)
criteria.fail: No relevant @type for page classification, or wrong @type

fix_template: Add or correct @type to match page classification
sources: @type tells search engines and AI systems what this content IS. Wrong @type = wrong interpretation. See schema-validation.md for type-to-page mapping.

name: @id With Unique Fragment URL
description: Verifies schema entities carry an @id using the {page URL}#{type} fragment pattern. @id enables entity linking across schema blocks (Organization ↔ Person ↔ Article), important for E-E-A-T entity graphs.

criteria.pass: Schema has `@id` field using `{page URL}#{type}` pattern (e.g., `"https://example.com/#organization"`)
criteria.warn: @id present but not using fragment pattern

sources: @id enables entity linking across schema blocks. Google uses @id to connect related entities (Organization ↔ Person ↔ Article). Particularly important for E-E-A-T entity graphs.

name: BreadcrumbList Present (Non-Homepage)
description: Verifies non-homepage pages carry BreadcrumbList schema with a valid itemListElement array. Breadcrumbs help search engines understand site hierarchy and enable breadcrumb rich results.

criteria.pass: BreadcrumbList schema with valid itemListElement array

fix_template: See schema-validation.md for BreadcrumbList required fields
sources: BreadcrumbList helps search engines understand site hierarchy. Enables breadcrumb rich results in SERPs.

name: Required Fields Present Per @type
description: Verifies each schema block carries all required fields for its @type per the schema-validation.md registry. Incomplete schema may fail Google Rich Results validation.

criteria.pass: All required fields present per schema-validation.md for each @type found
criteria.fail: 3+ required fields missing or critical fields (name, @type) missing

fix_template: Add missing required fields. See schema-validation.md for complete field list per @type.
sources: Incomplete schema may fail Google Rich Results validation. Generic/minimal schema underperforms no schema (41.6% vs 59.8% citation rate).

name: Recommended Fields Present Per @type
description: Verifies schema blocks include the recommended (not just required) fields for their @type. Attribute-rich schema earns a 61.7% citation rate — richness signals quality to AI systems.
sources: Attribute-rich schema earns 61.7% citation rate. Richness signals quality to AI systems.

name: Organization/WebSite Schema Present
description: Verifies Organization or WebSite schema exists. Organization schema establishes entity identity and WebSite schema enables sitelinks search — both matter for brand entity recognition by AI systems.

criteria.pass: Organization OR WebSite schema exists on the page
criteria.warn: Neither present on a non-homepage page (may exist on homepage only)

fix_template: Add Organization schema with name, url, logo, description, sameAs
sources: Organization schema establishes entity identity. WebSite schema enables sitelinks search. Both important for brand entity recognition by AI systems.

name: FAQPage Schema If FAQ Content Exists
description: Verifies that visible Q&A content is backed by matching FAQPage schema whose answers are complete and non-promotional. FAQPage yields a 3.2x citation increase, but Google may demote or reject markup with promotional or truncated answer text.
method_detail: If HTML has Q&A → check FAQPage schema + answer quality (no promotional, not truncated)

criteria.pass: HTML has Q&A content AND FAQPage schema exists matching it AND answer text contains no promotional/advertising content
criteria.warn: Q&A content exists but no FAQPage schema
criteria.fail: Mismatch: FAQPage schema exists but doesn't match visible Q&A content. Promotional: FAQ answer text contains promotional language, CTAs, pricing pitches, or advertising ("Buy now", "Sign up today", "Best in class"). Truncated: FAQ answers are truncated, incomplete, or too vague to be useful.

fix_template: Add FAQPage schema with mainEntity array matching visible Q&A pairs. Ensure each answer is: (a) factual and informative, not promotional, (b) complete — minimum 1-2 sentences, (c) self-contained — understandable without reading the rest of the page
sources: FAQPage = 3.2x citation increase in AI answers (BrightEdge). Sieve Rule #1603 (Schema.org, 0.97): "FAQPage Must Not Contain Promotional Content in Answers" — Google may demote or reject markup with promotional answer text. Sieve Rule #1602 (Schema.org, 0.96): "FAQPage Answer Text Must Not Be Truncated." Sieve AP#815 (high): "Including promotional or advertising content in FAQPage answer text." Sieve AP#816 (medium): "Writing truncated or incomplete FAQ answers."

name: Image Fields Use ImageObject
description: Verifies schema image fields use the ImageObject format rather than bare URL strings. ImageObject allows width, height, and caption — richer data for search engines; a minor quality signal.
method_detail: Check schema image fields for @type ImageObject

criteria.warn: Mix of ImageObject and bare URL strings
criteria.fail: All image fields are bare URL strings

sources: ImageObject allows width, height, caption — richer data for search engines. Minor quality signal.

name: datePublished/dateModified in ISO 8601
description: Verifies datePublished and dateModified are present in schema in ISO 8601 format. dateModified is the strongest freshness signal for AI engines; Perplexity weights freshness heavily.

criteria.pass: Both datePublished and dateModified present in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO)

fix_template: Add `"datePublished": "YYYY-MM-DD"` and `"dateModified": "YYYY-MM-DD"` (use actual dates, not placeholder)
sources: Google API leak: three date signals. Perplexity weights freshness heavily (3.2x for < 30 days). dateModified is the strongest freshness signal for AI engines.

name: Author With Person Type and Name
description: Verifies the schema author field is a Person object with a name, not a plain string. 96% of AI Overview citations come from E-E-A-T sources, and the author entity is a primary E-E-A-T signal.
method_detail: Check author field for Person @type + name

criteria.warn: Author field exists but as plain text string, not Person object

sources: 96% of AI Overview citations come from E-E-A-T sources. Author entity is a primary E-E-A-T signal. API leak confirmed `author` fields and `isAuthor` booleans stored per document.

name: Speakable Property Present
description: Verifies Article/WebPage schema carries a speakable SpeakableSpecification. Speakable markup tells voice assistants and AI extractors which sections are best for audio/answer extraction.
sources: Speakable markup tells voice assistants and AI extractors which sections are best for audio/answer extraction. Must target answer content, NOT navigation or CTAs.
